sentences.zh: 一二六四頁一行父蒯聵與輒爭國按：汲本、殿本「聵」作「瞶」。

translations.literal: Page 1264 line 1 "father Kuai Kui struggled with Ji for the state" — note: Jiben and Dianben write the name graph as "Gui" instead of "Kui."
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the palace editions use an alternate graph for Kuai Kui's name.

sentences.zh: 下同。

translations.literal: Same below.
translations.idiomatic: The same variant applies in following lines.

sentences.zh: 一二六四頁二行不以父命辭於王命按：陳景雲謂按公羊傳本文，當作「不以父命辭王父命」。

translations.literal: Page 1264 line 2 "does not reject the king's command on a father's order" — note: Chen Jingyun says checking the Guliang's original text, it ought to read "does not reject the king grandfather's command on a father's order."
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Chen Jingyun restores the Guliang wording with "grandfather's command."

sentences.zh: 一二六四頁一０行魯陽鄉在尋陽*

translations.literal: Page 1264 line 10 "Luyang township lies in Xunyang *
translations.idiomatic: Page 1264 line 10: lacuna in the Luyang geography note.

translations.literal: (commandery)
translations.idiomatic: Editorial mark: the following word should be commandery or county.

sentences.zh: **[縣]*也集解引洪亮吉說，謂漢時止有尋陽縣，屬廬江郡，此「郡」字蓋「縣」字之誤。

translations.literal: ** county **" also — the Jijie cites Hong Liangji saying Han times had only Xunyang county, belonging to Lujiang commandery; this "commandery" graph is probably error for "county."
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Hong Liangji shows that Han had Xunyang county in Lujiang, so "commandery" is a copyist's error for "county."

sentences.zh: 今據改。

translations.literal: Now accordingly changed.
translations.idiomatic: The text has been emended accordingly.

sentences.zh: 一二六四頁一三行數受賞賜擢徙校書刊誤謂漢校書者郎官而已，鴻已為二千石，不當以校書為擢徙也，明衍「校書」二字。

translations.literal: Page 1265 line 4 "repeatedly received rewards, promoted to collator" — Kanwu says Han collators were gentlemen only; Hong was already two-thousand-bushel rank — clearly redundant "collator" two graphs.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Kanwu doubts that a minister could be "promoted to collator" and suspects a two-character gloss crept into the text.

sentences.zh: 集解引惠棟說，謂如劉說，則「擢徙」二字無所附麗，或作「尚書」。

translations.literal: The Jijie cites Hui Dong saying if Liu's view, then "promoted transfer" two graphs have nothing to attach to — perhaps it reads "secretariat."
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Hui Dong suggests "secretariat" as an alternative if "collator" is struck.

sentences.zh: 校補謂案劉意，「擢徙」二字承上「數受賞賜」為一句，不必有所附麗，尚書六百石，亦非二千石擢徙之官。

translations.literal: The collation supplement says checking Liu's intent, "promoted transfer" two graphs continue "repeatedly received rewards" as one sentence, needing no further object; secretariat is six hundred bushels, also not an office a two-thousand-bushel man would be "promoted transfer" to.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: another editor argues "promoted transfer" simply follows "rewards" and need not govern an office name.

sentences.zh: 此傳但云「校書」，未言「校書郎」，則「賞賜擢徙」與「校書」各為一事，原不必校書定為官名。

translations.literal: This biography only says "collator," not "collating gentleman" — then "rewards promoted transfer" and "collator" are each separate matters; originally "collator" need not be fixed as an office title.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the biography may mean archival collation as a task, not the later title "collating gentleman."

sentences.zh: 今按：句當有脫鬥，諸說皆未諦。

translations.literal: Now checking: the sentence surely has missing graphs; the various theories are none exact.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the line is probably corrupt beyond recovery of one certain reading.

sentences.zh: 一二六五頁四行同斗斛權衡按：「同」原斗「角」，逕據汲本、殿本改正。

translations.literal: Page 1265 line 4 "unify bushels and balances" — note: "unify" was originally miswritten as "horn"; corrected from Jiben and Dianben.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the graph "unify" was miswritten as "horn" in some witnesses.

sentences.zh: 一二六五頁一二行弒君三十二按：「弒」原斗「殺」，逕據汲本、殿本改正。

translations.literal: Page 1265 line 12 "ruler-murders thirty-two" — note: "murder ruler" was originally miswritten as "kill"; corrected from Jiben and Dianben.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the text distinguishes regicide from ordinary killing.

sentences.zh: 一二六六頁一一行有伊尹之心則可無伊尹之心則篡也按：殿本「心」皆改作「志」，取與今本孟子合。

translations.literal: Page 1266 line 11 "if one has Yi Yin's heart it is acceptable, without Yi Yin's heart it is usurpation" — note: Dianben changes both "heart" to "will" to match today's Mencius.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the Dianben harmonizes the quotation with the received Mencius by reading "will" instead of "heart."

sentences.zh: 校補謂案周章傳論已引作「心」，官本同，周廣業據為孟子異本是也。

translations.literal: The collation supplement says checking the Zhou Zhang biography's judgment, it already quotes "heart," matching the official edition — Zhou Guangye rightly cites it as a variant Mencius.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: an earlier Hou Han judgment already used "heart," so the graph may be an authentic variant of Mencius.

sentences.zh: 一二六六頁一四行雖有隱謀按：集解引王補說，謂袁宏紀作「雖欲隱諱」。

translations.literal: Page 1266 line 14 "though there be hidden plots" — note: the Jijie cites Wang Bu saying Yuan Hong's annals read "though wishing to hide."
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Yuan Hong's text reads "though wishing to conceal" instead of "hidden plots."

sentences.zh: 一二六七頁二行東觀記亦*

translations.literal: Page 1267 line 2 "the Eastern Lodge Record also *
translations.idiomatic: Page 1267 line 2: lacuna in the Eastern Lodge quotation.

translations.literal: (says)
translations.idiomatic: Editorial restoration of the verb "says."

sentences.zh: *作先節據校補刪。

translations.literal: *writes before season" — deleted per collation supplement.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: a spurious phrase "writes before season" has been removed.

sentences.zh: 一二六七頁一三行左傳曰天為剛德按：汲本、殿本注無此七字，而有「天道終日干干是其剛也」十字。

translations.literal: Page 1267 line 13 "the Zuo commentary says Heaven has a firm virtue" — note: Jiben and Dianben commentary lack these seven graphs but have ten graphs "Heaven's way ends in day; day day is its firmness."
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the palace editions substitute a longer gloss on Heaven's firmness for the short Zuo quotation.

sentences.zh: 一二六八頁五行*[湛]*卒子浮嗣。

translations.literal: Page 1268 line 5 "*[Zhan]* died; his son Fu succeeded."
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the heir's name Zhan is restored from Jiben and Dianben.

sentences.zh: 據汲本、殿本補。

translations.literal: Supplied from Jiben and Dianben.
translations.idiomatic: The restoration follows the palace block prints.

sentences.zh: 一二六八頁一０行而己厚其名按：集解引惠棟說，謂華嶠書「厚」作「享」。

translations.literal: Page 1268 line 10 "while they thickened their own name" — note: the Jijie cites Hui Dong saying Hua Qiao's book writes "enjoy" for "thicken."
translations.idiomatic: Collation: Hua Qiao's history uses a different graph meaning "to enjoy" where this text has "thicken" or "fatten."
